economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Exchange Income Corporation ($1,000,000), Wasaya Airways Limited Partnership, Wasaya Group Inc., and Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership. Deposit of $916,667 related to potential purchase of L.K.G.H. Group of Companies sawmill. Construction in progress of $20,496,224. Youth Sawmill Program active. Economic dependence on ISC funding noted.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to improper deferral of revenue under fixed contribution funding agreements - auditors note that had revenue not been deferred, accumulated surplus would be $32,501,359 higher and deferred revenue correspondingly lower. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Pikangikum Health Authority, Pikangikum CMHC Housing Authority, Whitefeather Forest Management Corporation. Deferred revenue balance is very large at $44,204,590 relative to total revenue. First Nation is not in compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency and Accountability Act filing deadline. CMHC replacement reserve was underfunded by $688,752. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable ($8,115,819) plus due to government ($2,767,047). Total government transfers calculated as all federal and provincial government transfer lines. Capital segment expenses of $6,103,470 classified under infrastructure.
